title: Requests, Limits, QoS, Probes, Startup, Readiness, and Resource Failure · Object and Event Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective health and resource behavior policy; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in requests, limits, qos, probes, startup, readiness, and resource failure, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in health and resource behavior policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Relate CPU and memory requests to scheduling and accounting, limits to runtime enforcement, and application concurrency to measured capacity.
Derive Guaranteed, Burstable, and BestEffort QoS, container OOM, Pod eviction, node pressure, and restart evidence from effective resources.
Separate startup gating, traffic readiness, process liveness, container restart, endpoint conditions, and dependency health.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Requests Limits Scheduling Runtime
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug service passes liveness while serving stale data, has no startup probe, requests 100m but uses 1250m CPU, and is OOMKilled under pressure. Redesign probes, requests, limits, QoS, graceful termination, and changed-load checks. Competency probe: Relate CPU and memory requests to scheduling and accounting, limits to runtime enforcement, and application concurrency to measured capacity.

Inspect the K8s Requests Limits Scheduling Runtime trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-resource-probe-fault-tests-go7mse". Tailor its health-resource-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests, keep K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-resource-probe-fault-tests-go7mse
  - competency: k8s-resource-probe-fault-tests
    artifact: health-resource-policy.yaml
    decision: resource probe fault tests
    environment: kubeadm recovery cluster restored onto replacement nodes
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: Pod state previous exit endpoint readiness and request result
    case-key: go7mse
    change: |
      requests: {cpu: 200m, memory: 256Mi}
      limits: {memory: 512Mi}
      startupProbe: bounded-cold-start
      readinessProbe: traffic-contract
      livenessProbe: process-deadlock-only
      fault-test: throttling-and-oom
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl top pod status-api --containers
    failure: Reject dependency-based liveness, requests as runtime caps, green idle probes as capacity proof, or unexplained OOM and eviction.
    repair: Measure workload demand, separate probe purposes, calculate QoS, inject startup and pressure faults, and correlate container and node evidence.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests structural check is only one gate. health and resource behavior policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test cold start, overload, dependency loss, memory pressure, CPU throttling, termination, and recovery without turning probes into cascading failure.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug service passes liveness while serving stale data, has no startup probe, requests 100m but uses 750m CPU, and is OOMKilled under pressure. Redesign probes, requests, limits, QoS, graceful termination, and changed-load checks. Competency probe: Test cold start, overload, dependency loss, memory pressure, CPU throttling, termination, and recovery without turning probes into cascading failure.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A green readiness check under one idle run proves production capacity and resilience.

checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and cold plus specific case evidence.
